What is Cyber Security?

Imagine your house. You lock the doors, secure the windows, and maybe even have a security system to keep it safe. Well, cybersecurity is like doing the same thing for your digital life.

When you use a computer, smartphone, or any device connected to the internet, you're part of a vast, digital world. This world is full of information, from your personal photos and bank details to important company secrets.

Cybersecurity is about protecting this digital world from harm. This includes preventing hackers from stealing your information, disrupting your devices, or even causing damage to online systems.

Think of it like a digital shield. It helps keep your online activities safe and prevents unwanted intruders from entering your digital space.

How Does Cyber Security Apply to Small Businesses?

Just like a small business needs a strong foundation to grow, it also needs a strong digital foundation. Cybersecurity is that foundation.

Even though small businesses might not have the same resources as big corporations, they're still vulnerable to cyber threats. Hackers often target smaller businesses because they might have weaker security measures.

Why is it Important?

  • Protecting Customer Data: If a customer's information is stolen, it can damage your reputation and lead to lost business.
  • Preventing Disruptions: A cyberattack can cripple your operations, leading to financial losses and customer dissatisfaction.
  • Complying with Regulations: Many industries have data privacy regulations that small businesses must follow.
  • Safeguarding Intellectual Property: Your business ideas, designs, and other valuable assets are protected by cybersecurity.

Statistics

46%

Of all breaches target small businesses

61% of SMBs were hit by a cyberattack in 2021. Small businesses are seen as easier targets due to weaker security measures.

350%

Increase in social engineering attacks

Employees of small businesses experience more social engineering attacks (like phishing) than those at larger enterprises.

82%

Of ransomware attacks target small businesses

37% of companies hit by ransomware had fewer than 100 employees. Small businesses are often less prepared to recover from ransomware attacks.

$117,000

Is the average cost of a data breach

This can be devastating for many small businesses.

Cyber Security Services

Small Business 1-10 Employees

  • Firewall configuration and monitoring
  • Security Patch Management
  • Basic Employee Security Training
  • Data Protection & Backups
  • Email Filtering & Anti-Phishing
  • Password Management Recommendations

Growing Business 11-25 Employees

  • All Small Business Services, Plus:
  • Enhanced Network Monitoring
  • Detailed Threat Reports
  • Regular Security Assessments
  • Secure Remote Access Setup
  • Data Encryption*
  • Incident Response Support

Medium Business 26-50 Employees

  • All Growing Business Services, Plus:
  • Advanced Threat Detection
  • Penetration Testing
  • Policy & Compliance Management
  • Endpoint Protection
  • Secure File Sharing & Collaboration
  • Security Awareness Training Sessions

Established Business 51-100 Employees

  • All Medium Business Services, Plus:
  • 24/7 Security Monitoring
  • Advanced Incident Response
  • Network Segmentation & Access Control
  • Security Operations Center Integration
  • Disaster Recovery Planning
  • Vendor Security Management

Large Business 100+ Employees

  • All Established Business Services, Plus:
  • Comprehensive SOC Services
  • Security Information & Event Management
  • Compliance Consulting & Audit Support
  • Zero Trust Architecture Implementation
  • Cloud Security Management
  • Custom Security Solutions

IT Services

Small Business 1-10 Employees

  • Onsite Server/Computer Setup & Maintenance
  • Basic Networking Setup & Maintenance
  • Basic Troubleshooting & Tech Support
  • System Health Monitoring
  • Optional Security System Setup

Growing Business 11-25 Employees

  • All Small Business Services, Plus:
  • Advanced Networking Installation & Maintenance
  • Server Configuration & Virtualization
  • Proactive Troubleshooting
  • Workstation Lifecycle Management
  • Data Backup & Recovery
  • Optional Security System Setup & Maintenance

Medium Business 26-50 Employees

  • All Growing Business Services, Plus:
  • Comprehensive Network Design & Support
  • Server Maintenance & Support
  • Advanced Troubleshooting & Onsite Support
  • Cloud Integration & Support
  • IT Policy Development & Compliance Support
  • Optional Security System Setup & Advanced Configuration

Established Business 51-100 Employees

  • All Medium Business Services, Plus:
  • Full IT Infrastructure Management
  • Network Security & Advanced Troubleshooting
  • Help Desk & Ticketing System
  • Project Management & Consulting
  • Advanced Cloud Solutions
  • Optional Security System Setup, but No Monitoring

Large Business 100+ Employees

  • All Established Business Services, Plus:
  • Full IT Department Management
  • Data Center & Virtualization Management
  • Advanced Network Design & Multi-Site Support
  • Vendor Management & Strategic Partnerships
  • Ongoing IT Consultation & Support
  • Optional Security Systems for Basic Setup Only

The Lore

My name is Dorian Balogh, and I have been working with computers and electronics for over years. Ever since I can remember, I have had a passion for taking apart anything and everything to understand how it works. This curiosity led me into the tech industry, starting with cell phones. After spending some time at a small kiosk, I transitioned to a role as an administrative worker at a small computer repair shop. Unfortunately, this position was short-lived as the shop couldn’t sustain so many employees.

I then sought other opportunities in my field, but times were tough, and I struggled to find work. After relocating to a new city, I secured two jobs—one in security and the other in cell phones. I held these positions for several years, gradually building my knowledge of security and recognizing the vulnerabilities in technology. During this period, massive hacks occurred almost weekly, which deeply concerned me. This realization drove me to explore server work and self-hosting to ensure that if I were ever hacked, I could only blame myself.

As the years went by, I took on various roles, even becoming a regional manager for Samsung’s cell phone department. However, that position didn’t last long, as a devastating software update impacted the entire human race for a couple of years. During this challenging time, my father encouraged me to pursue higher education. I applied to university and was accepted just a week later to study for my bachelor’s degree in computer science.

Fast forward to 2024, and I am still working as a security guard when a friend suggests, “Why don’t you just start your own company?” Always wanting to work for myself, I thought, why not? This brings us to today, where, with five years of server experience and six years of cyber security research and knowledge, along with a burning passion for the right to repair, I present to you CyberConnect—where technology meets security.
